principles: Prefer user-intent views over generic taxonomy labels.
A resource may belong to multiple views.
Split videos/collections from single source articles when meaningful.
Use evidence-backed confidence and expose uncertainty.
Avoid creating tiny views with fewer than three resources unless the user requested them.
User tags and user descriptions are high-priority evidence, not cosmetic labels.
Views are semantic lenses; do not force resources into one permanent folder.
Support strict/loose/purpose-based commands by changing inclusion and exclusion rules, not by changing the underlying resource.
When a command says 'mostly X but welcome Y', create sections or membership states rather than excluding Y.
If the model is unsure, create a focused review queue instead of pretending certainty.
evidenceRules: Explicit user descriptions outrank title/URL and AI-inferred tags.
Explicit user tags must be included in resource briefs before extracted metadata.
Every membership needs at least one evidence reference.
Transcript-derived claims must reference a transcript artifact.
Description-derived claims must reference a description artifact.
Title-only classifications must be marked low or medium confidence unless the URL/host strongly supports them.
